Vision/product-vision.md.author: Alice Chen

anchor.quotedText: What has changed in the market, technology, or user behavior that makes this the right time to build?

Vision/product-vision.md.text: I'd push for something more concrete here — e.g., AI coding assistants, remote-first teams normalizing async reviews. "Right time" only lands if it's tied to a specific shift.

Vision/product-vision.md.author: Alice Chen

anchor.quotedText: Describe your primary user. Be specific — a narrow, vivid description beats a broad one.

Vision/product-vision.md.text: Agree completely. What about B2B PMs at 50–200 person companies who don't have a dedicated design partner? That's who I see struggling most with pre-build alignment.

Strategy/strategy.md.author: Marcus Johnson

anchor.quotedText: What must be true for this strategy to work? These are your biggest risks.

Strategy/strategy.md.text: This is the most important section and it's empty in every doc I've seen. Suggest we make it required before a doc can be committed. Would make a good constraint to enforce at the git hook level.

Strategy/competitive-analysis.md.author: Alice Chen

anchor.quotedText: What can we do that alternatives cannot? Be honest — if the answer is "nothing yet," write that.

Strategy/competitive-analysis.md.text: Love that the template forces honesty here. Our real differentiator is the git model — every other PM tool hides version history.

Planning/roadmap.md.author: Marcus Johnson

anchor.quotedText: Things we've considered and actively chosen not to do yet. Write the reason.

Planning/roadmap.md.text: The "Deprioritized" section is criminally underused in roadmaps. This template is already better than Notion for making tradeoffs visible.

Discovery/user-research.md.author: Alice Chen

anchor.quotedText: What did we learn? Be specific — name patterns, quote users, cite data.

Discovery/user-research.md.text: Big +1 on citing actual quotes. We rarely do this and then wonder why stakeholders don't believe the research.
